task_completion.description: Agent constructs an XML payload with the correct structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor, script, or terminal command containing XML with a <map> root element, <entry> elements, <string> keys, and <com.mirth.connect.util.ConfigurationProperty> elements containing <value> and <comment>.

task_completion.description: XML payload includes the required facility and HL7 entries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML payload includes entries for FACILITY_CODE (MCH001), FACILITY_NAME (Mercy Community Hospital), SENDING_APPLICATION (NEXTGEN_CONNECT), and SENDING_FACILITY (MCH).

task_completion.description: XML payload includes the required network and environment entries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML payload includes entries for ADT_DESTINATION_HOST (10.10.50.25), ADT_DESTINATION_PORT (6661), ORM_DESTINATION_HOST (10.10.50.30), ORM_DESTINATION_PORT (6662), ENVIRONMENT (PRODUCTION), and RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS (5).

task_completion.description: Agent configures the PUT request with the correct endpoint, headers, and credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a curl command, Python script, or API client configured to send a PUT request to 'https://localhost:8443/api/server/configurationMap' with the header 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' and authentication (admin/admin).

task_completion.description: Agent executes the API request and receives a successful response.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the terminal or API client executing the request. Look for a successful HTTP response (e.g., HTTP 200 OK, or no error message returned by curl) indicating the Configuration Map was updated.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the REST API as requested, rather than the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the terminal (e.g., curl, python) or an API client to complete the task, not the Mirth Connect Administrator Java GUI.

integrity.description: Verifies the API request was actually executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the command being run and returning a response, rather than just being typed out without execution.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just print a fake success message.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output of the script or curl command looks like a genuine network response or standard script execution, rather than a simple hardcoded 'echo Success'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Configuration Map requires 10 specific entries. Key pairs include: FACILITY_CODE: MCH001, FACILITY_NAME: Mercy Community Hospital, SENDING_APPLICATION: NEXTGEN_CONNECT, SENDING_FACILITY: MCH, ADT_DESTINATION_HOST: 10.10.50.25, ADT_DESTINATION_PORT: 6661, ORM_DESTINATION_HOST: 10.10.50.30, ORM_DESTINATION_PORT: 6662, ENVIRONMENT: PRODUCTION, RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS: 5. The payload must be XML with a <map> root, <entry> elements containing <string> keys, and <com.mirth.connect.util.ConfigurationProperty> containing <value> and <comment>.
